user_message: Karen Berg at Iron Creek Mining just sent this. Help me map the stakeholders.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-01-17
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:08:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:18:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:23:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:26:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:28:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:31:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:33:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Iron Creek Mining: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF48-O trap (No native 'roadmap-to-forecast' workflow primitive); produces complete Roadmap-to-forecast mapping, milestone date proposals, opportunity sequencing, new opportunity propo
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF48-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF48-O failure mode (No native 'roadmap-to-forecast' workflow primitive)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF48-O -- No native 'roadmap-to-forecast' workflow primitive
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
